title: The information in this document is provided as a guide only
text:
Tax and Duty Manual                                                     Pensions Manual - Chapter 14
The information in this document is provided as a guide only 
and is not professional advice, including legal advice. It should 
not be assumed that the guidance is comprehensive or that it 
provides a definitive answer in every case.
1
Discontinuance of Schemes
Chapter 14
This chapter should be read in conjunction with Part 30, Chapter 1 of the Taxes 
Consolidation Act 1997.
Document last updated January 2026
Tax and Duty Manual                                                     Pensions Manual - Chapter 14
2
Table of Contents
1 General...........................................................................................................................3
2 Frozen or paid-up schemes ............................................................................................3
3 Winding-up.....................................................................................................................3
4 Tax..................................................................................................................................4
5 Surplus on winding-up ...................................................................................................4
6 Partial winding-up ..........................................................................................................4
7 Discontinuance at interim stage ....................................................................................4
8 Revenue contact ............................................................................................................5
Tax and Duty Manual                                                     Pensions Manual - Chapter 14
3
1 General
Approved pension schemes may be discontinued in a number of circumstances, such as the 
employer ceasing to contribute to the scheme, or the bankruptcy or liquidation of the 
employer. The fact that scheme rules may allow an employer to discontinue a scheme does 
not constitute a breach of the requirement that an exempt approved scheme must be 
established under irrevocable trusts.  
Revenue should be notified of a scheme's discontinuance in routine cases within three 
months after the event1. However, Revenue should be informed at an earlier stage in any of 
the following circumstances:
 if the scheme to be discontinued is not yet approved;
 if it is proposed to refund monies to the employer(s);
 if a block transfer is to be made.
A scheme may be discontinued either by making it making it frozen or “paid up”, or by 
winding-up the scheme. Scheme rules must specify the action to be taken on 
discontinuance.
2 Frozen or paid-up schemes
A scheme is paid up when all contributions cease, but the assets of the scheme continue to 
be held by an administrator to be applied in accordance with the rules of the scheme. A 
scheme may continue to exist in a paid-up form despite the employer ceasing to exist, but 
this must be done with prior approval from Revenue. This agreement is unlikely if the 
scheme is in surplus, or there is a strong possibility that a surplus will arise in the future. A 
paid-up scheme must eventually wind up when no assets or beneficiaries remain.
3 Winding-up
A scheme may be wound up as soon as it is decided to discontinue it.  Where assets and 
liabilities match, the winding-up may be done by:
 transfer to another approved scheme or to a PRSA;
 securing the benefits under individual buy-out bonds; or
 assigning individual scheme policies to the members.
1 Schedule 23, Part 1, Paragraph 3(2)(a) Taxes Consolidation Act 1997 (TCA).
Tax and Duty Manual                                                     Pensions Manual - Chapter 14
4
Where the scheme is wound up, the benefits provided should not exceed the maximum 
approvable if the employee concerned had withdrawn from service on the date when the 
benefits were determined - see Chapter 12 for further details on withdrawal from service.
The winding-up rule should not provide for payment of a lump sum in lieu of the purchase 
of a deferred pension, or for an optional refund of the employee's contributions to any 
employee who is in service when the scheme begins to be wound up.
The rules should also ensure that deferred benefits whether in pension or lump sum form, 
should not be received earlier than the earliest date from which an immediate pension on 
early retirement could be paid under the rules of the scheme.
4 Tax
A pension scheme should not have a rule permitting full commutation of a pension on 
grounds of triviality or the pensioner’s subsequent ill-health, unless the life office or pension 
provider concerned has first made satisfactory arrangements for any payment of tax due.
5 Surplus on winding-up
If the assets available are clearly more than sufficient to provide all prospective benefits, the 
rules may provide additional benefits within approvable limits. There must be express 
provision for the return of any remaining surplus to the employer as soon as the liabilities of 
the scheme are determined and satisfied. The amount returned to the employer is liable to 
tax under section 782 Taxes Consolidation Act 1997 (TCA).
In the case of a group fund, the surplus should be divided among the existing employers on 
the contribution basis or such other arrangement that has been agreed with Revenue.
6 Partial winding-up
A scheme which provides for the participation of more than one employer should provide 
for that part of the scheme relating to any participating employer to be wound up if that 
employer goes out of business or leaves the scheme for other reasons. The same 
considerations apply to a partial winding-up as to a total winding-up.
7 Discontinuance at interim stage
Revenue must be informed of the discontinuance of a scheme where a definitive trust deed 
is not in place and the scheme is operating under an interim trust deed.
Tax and Duty Manual                                                     Pensions Manual - Chapter 14
5
8 Revenue contact
Where this manual instructs that Revenue should be informed, all correspondence should 
be addressed to:
Office of the Revenue Commissioners
High Wealth and Financial Services Division
Pensions Branch
Castle View
52 – 57 South Great George’s Street
Dublin 2
D02 HF50
For email, please use the secure “MyEnquiries” service available in myAccount or Revenue 
Online Service (ROS).
